comment: LEGAL FLOOR for the form-publish pipeline (Brief 1 PR-1). publishValidation.ts only
checks STRUCTURE (a signature_table IF PRESENT has one signature column; a legal
static_text IF PRESENT is well-formed) — nothing made a JHA keep its 'REVIEW AND REVISE
THE PLAN' footer or an equipment form keep its lock/tag-out line. This manifest is that
missing requirement, enforced at BOTH the C3 layers: the Worker enqueue gate
(worker/publishValidation.ts validateDefinition) and the Mac daemon's authoritative
re-check vs live HEAD (safety_reports/publish_manifest.apply_publish).
APPLIES to create / edit / add_version (they carry a definition). delete / rollback are
unaffected (rollback returns to a historically-valid version).
EFFECTIVE SPEC for a definition = parents[parent_form_code] shallow-merged with
identities[identity] (identity keys win on collision). If NEITHER a parent nor an identity
entry exists (a brand-new form type under a brand-new parent), defaults_for_new_identities
applies — so the demonstrated operator create-new-type flow keeps working while the strict
entries protect the legal forms.
CHANGING A STRICT ENTRY IS A LEGAL-FLOOR (doctrine-adjacent) CHANGE: high-capability-class,
Tier-3/Seth only — the Successor-Operator must NOT edit this file. See
docs/runbooks/safety_portal_forms.md.
Equipment signature: required at the equipment-preinspection PARENT (min 1) — every variant
(skid-steer, telehandler) carries an operator-signature input. Seth-confirmed 2026-06-10; a
signature field was added to equipment-telehandler-v1 in the same PR to close the prior gap.

spec_keys.required_section_types: array of section `type` values (e.g. signature_table, content_blocks) at least one of EACH must be present
spec_keys.required_signature_inputs_min: minimum count of fields/columns with input:'signature' across all sections
spec_keys.required_static_text: array of byte-exact substrings, EACH must appear in some static_text whose emphasis is 'legal' or 'footer'
spec_keys.required_field_keys: array of field keys (anywhere) that must be present

required_static_text: ANY LIFT EXCEEDING 75% OF CRANE RATED CAPACITY IS A CRITICAL LIFT
DOCUMENT RETENTION: Retain completed lift plan for minimum 3 years
All parties below must be signed before lift commences

comment: [OPERATOR-CONFIRMED 2026-07-03 (proceed-with-defaults directive) — SOP daily form D1] Progress form with NO
signature requirements. required_field_keys is the DFR legal floor CARRIED FROM v1 into
the SOP-guided v2: the data keys BOTH versions share. It is deliberately the v1∩v2 set —
this spec is identity-keyed, so every daily-report-*.json (v1 AND v2) must satisfy it
(tests/test_form_definitions.py applies it to each shipped version). v1-only keys
(job_name, report_date) moved to the submission envelope (job / work_date) in v2 and
therefore cannot be required here; v2-only additions (guidance/form_link sections, the
SOP duty confirms) likewise cannot. The floor guarantees no DFR data field is lost by a
future edit of either version.
[Slice 1 amputation guard, 2026-07-03 — R3-F3] required_section_types floors the D4/M2
MOUNTS (job_requirements + expected_materials): a form-builder edit can never silently
amputate the per-job requirements / expected-materials sections — both C3 layers
(worker/publishValidation.ts + publish_manifest.check_required_content) reject a NEW
daily-report definition missing either mount. This is the one floor key that is NOT the
v1∩v2 intersection: shipped HISTORICAL versions (v1-v4) predate the mounts, are not
editor-reachable (the builder opens only current_form_code), and re-enter service only
via rollback (deliberately floor-exempt) — so tests/test_form_definitions.py exempts
non-current shipped versions from required_section_types ONLY; every publish-time check
applies the full floor.
[OPERATOR-CONFIRMED 2026-07-03 (proceed-with-defaults directive; category: progress stands) — Material receipts M2] Progress-category
(commercial) form with NO signature requirement — strict entry per the M2 protocol,
flagged for operator veto alongside the category choice. The floor is the incident's
evidentiary minimum: WHAT material (material_description), WHAT KIND of problem
(issue), and the free-text account (details). qty fields / delivery_ref / photos /
action_taken are deliberately NOT floored — a future edit may reshape them without a
legal-floor change.
